title: AI uses less water than the public thinks
summary: The article debunks the widespread misconception that AI systems consume large amounts of water, clarifying that while training large models does require cooling (and thus water), total AI-related water use remains negligible compared to agriculture or power generation—especially in drought-prone California. It emphasizes data-driven quantification over sensationalism and draws policy-relevant lessons about resource trade-offs in tech infrastructure. The analysis serves as a corrective case study for sustainable AI deployment planning.
key_insight: AI的环境影响需放在全系统资源消耗背景下评估，孤立夸大其水足迹会扭曲技术治理优先级。
